query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity:

## Key Findings (latest through June 30, 2026)

**Record H1 2026 selloff:** Foreign investors were net sellers of **~148.3–149.0 trillion won** (~$95–97B) of KOSPI shares in the first half — the **largest first-half net outflow on record** — even as the KOSPI hit record highs (8,476.48 on June 30, ~+101% YTD).

**Monthly:**
- May: ~44.7T won (KOSPI); 47+T won across all domestic stocks (FSS record)
- June: ~48.6T won (heaviest month)
- May–June combined: ~92.9T won

**Recent weekly/daily trend (the crux of the question):**
- A **24-trading-day selling streak** worth 75.569T won (4th-longest ever), starting ~June 3
- Brief **reversal to net BUYING of ~1.7T won on June 12** (KOSPI +8% on Middle East war-end hopes)
- Renewed selling for **8 consecutive days from June 19**
- **Record single-day net sell of 7.73–7.76T won on June 29/30**

**Drivers:** Profit-taking/rebalancing after the ~101% surge, won weakness (~1,549/USD), and rotation into Japanese equities. Selling concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK hynix. Notably, foreign ownership still hit an all-time high >35% due to price appreciation. Brokerages expect the net-selling trend to persist into H2 2026.

A note on data vintage: figures vary slightly by source due to KOSPI-only vs. all-market coverage and settlement-date conventions. This is factual reporting only — no forecasting.

key_facts: H1 2026 foreign net selling on KOSPI: ~148.3–149.0 trillion won (~$95–97B), largest first-half net outflow on record.
June 2026 KOSPI foreign net selling: ~48.6 trillion won (heaviest month of H1).
May 2026 KOSPI foreign net selling: ~44.7 trillion won; all-domestic-stocks figure (FSS) was 47+ trillion won, a record.
May–June 2026 combined foreign net selling: ~92.89 trillion won.
Record single-day foreign net sell: 7.73–7.76 trillion won on June 29/30, 2026 — largest daily net sale on record.
Early-June selling streak: 75.569 trillion won net sold over 24 consecutive trading days (4th-longest streak; began ~June 3).
Brief reversal: net BUYING of ~1.7 trillion won on June 12, 2026 (first positive day in ~a month) as KOSPI surged >8% on Mideast war-end expectations.
Renewed selling: 8 consecutive trading days of foreign net selling from June 19 into quarter-end.
Selling concentrated in Samsung Electronics (~72.6T won H1) and SK hynix (~57.1T won H1); Samsung foreign ownership hit 16-year low.
Individuals net-bought ~99.17T won and institutions ~35T won in H1 2026, absorbing foreign selling.
KOSPI closed at 8,476.48 on June 30, 2026 (+0.97%); index rose ~101% in H1 2026.
Foreign ownership ratio rose to all-time high above 35% despite record selling, due to price appreciation.
Drivers cited: profit-taking/rebalancing after surge, won weakness (~1,549 won/USD), and rotation into Japanese equities.
